system_prompt: You are a Minecraft reconstruction planner. Return only one strict JSON object with a compact build plan.
prompt:
You are given a textual building description.
Create an executable build plan using ONLY these operation types:
- fill: axis-aligned cuboid fill
- carve: axis-aligned cuboid set to air
- set: single block placement

Output STRICT JSON with this schema:
{
  "plan_version": "v1",
  "bbox": {"xmin": 0, "xmax": 0, "ymin": 0, "ymax": 0, "zmin": 0, "zmax": 0},
  "palette": {
    "wall": "stonebrick",
    "roof": "wood",
    "trim": "brick",
    "glass": "glass",
    "light": "glowstone",
    "floor": "wood"
  },
  "operations": [
    {"op": "fill", "x1": 0, "y1": 0, "z1": 0, "x2": 1, "y2": 1, "z2": 1, "block": "stonebrick", "purpose": "base"},
    {"op": "carve", "x1": 0, "y1": 0, "z1": 0, "x2": 0, "y2": 0, "z2": 0, "purpose": "hollow"},
    {"op": "set", "x": 0, "y": 0, "z": 0, "block": "glowstone", "purpose": "light"}
  ],
  "notes": ["optional"]
}

Rules:
- Coordinates must be integers.
- Keep bbox anchored near origin and positive when possible.
- operations count <= 260.
- Create a complete, coherent building: floor, walls, roof, windows, entrance.
- Use normalized material IDs when possible.

Description JSON:
{
  "summary": "Two-story rectangular brick building with a dark stepped gable roof, stone/white trim base and cornices, horizontal wooden band between floors, regular rows of small glass windows, and corner stone pillars. Sits on a raised white slab border around a dirt foundation.",
  "building_type": "residential_block_like",
  "shape": {
    "overall_form": "simple rectangular prism with a stepped gable roof",
    "footprint_hint": "rectangle",
    "symmetry": "bilaterally symmetric along the long center axis (front/back mirrored)",
    "floors_estimate": 2,
    "roof_type": "gable"
  },
  "dimensions_estimate": {
    "width": 9,
    "depth": 17,
    "height": 9
  },
  "materials": [
    {
      "name": "brick",
      "role": "wall",
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "name": "nether_brick",
      "role": "roof",
      "confidence": 0.9
    },
    {
      "name": "quartz_block",
      "role": "base/cornice/trim",
      "confidence": 0.85
    },
    {
      "name": "oak_planks",
      "role": "horizontal band/ledges",
      "confidence": 0.8
    },
    {
      "name": "stonebrick",
      "role": "corner pillar/vertical trim",
      "confidence": 0.6
    },
    {
      "name": "glass",
      "role": "windows",
      "confidence": 0.98
    }
  ],
  "elements": [
    "rows of small glass windows on both floors (repeating pattern)",
    "entrance not visible (uncertain) - likely on opposite/side face",
    "stepped dark gable roof with quartz cornice accents",
    "raised quartz slab border foundation around perimeter",
    "wooden horizontal band between first and second floor",
    "stone/stonebrick corner pillars"
  ],
  "rebuild_hints": [
    "Create a rectangular two-floor box of brick with corners reinforced with stonebrick columns (3-high corners visible).",
    "Add a continuous oak plank band around mid-height between floors and place a quartz slab/cornice above and below the band.",
    "Place repeating 1x1 or 1x2 glass windows in a regular row on each floor (spacing consistent across faces).",
    "Construct a stepped dark nether_brick gable roof centered on the long axis, add quartz block caps at the eaves corners and small quartz slab highlights on steps.",
    "Surround the building with a one-block-high quartz slab/blocks border raised above the dirt ground as shown."
  ],
  "uncertainties": [
    "Exact interior layout and presence/location of doors are not visible from provided exterior images.",
    "Material IDs: quartz vs. smooth_sandstone or diorite could be used for white trim; confidence given but not certain.",
    "Corner vertical blocks may be stonebrick or andesite; identification is medium confidence.",
    "Exact block counts and precise footprint dimensions estimated from perspective/top views; actual counts may differ by a few blocks."
  ],
  "provider": "openai",
  "model": "gpt-5-mini",
  "building": "building_000",
  "created_at": "2026-02-28T13:56:48.672181+00:00"
}
